title: Nia's Colorful Feelings
synopsis: Little Nia discovers how her feelings have different colors as she experiences happiness, sadness, and anger throughout her day.

pages.text: This is Nia. She feels happy! Happy is bright yellow like sunshine.

illustration.description: Nia with long braids with colorful beads, dark brown skin, warm kind eyes stands in bright yellow sunshine swirls, abstract happy emotion background with golden rays and cheerful yellow clouds
illustration.alt: Happy girl surrounded by yellow sunshine colors

pages.text: Now Nia feels sad. Sad is blue like rain drops falling down.

illustration.description: Nia with long braids with colorful beads, dark brown skin, warm kind eyes surrounded by sad blue rain drops and blue swirls, abstract melancholy emotion background with gentle blue tears
illustration.alt: Sad girl with blue rain around her

pages.text: Nia feels angry! Angry is red like fire swirling all around.

illustration.description: Nia with long braids with colorful beads, dark brown skin, warm kind eyes surrounded by angry red swirls and flame-like patterns, abstract anger emotion background with red zigzags
illustration.alt: Angry girl with red swirls around her

pages.text: All feelings are okay. Yellow happy, blue sad, red angry. Nia loves her colorful feelings!

illustration.description: Nia with long braids with colorful beads, dark brown skin, warm kind eyes standing peacefully among swirling yellow, blue, and red emotion colors blending harmoniously in abstract rainbow emotion background
illustration.alt: Girl surrounded by all feeling colors together

metadata.illustrationStyle: emotion mapped colors, angry red swirls, sad blue rain, happy yellow sunshine, abstract emotion backgrounds, Inside Out inspired, feeling visualization, color psychology

questions.question: What color is happy?
questions.explanation: Happy feelings are bright yellow like sunshine!

questions.question: What color is sad?
questions.explanation: Sad feelings are blue like rain drops.

questions.question: Are all feelings okay?
questions.explanation: Yes! All feelings are okay to have.
